rulings.Case Title: DENNIS MCDANIEL et al VS. TELEGRAPH LANDING NORTH ASSOCIATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 6, 2009, line 3, DEFENDANT TELEGRAPH LANDING NORTH ASSOCIATION MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Motion for summary adjudication granted as to injunctive relief, which is now moot, and the cause of action for negligent infliction of emotional distress. No evidence of "serious emotional distress." Motion denied as to cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty, negligence and nuisance claims. Triable issue of fact raised whether the association breached a duty to plaintiffs to maintain the common areas. (UMF 9). Defendant hired firms to deal with the leak and asbestos issues, and there were delays not attributable to plaintiff in completing the repairs. Whether the Association could have remedied the problems more quickly is a question of fact. There is also the triable issue of fact regarding damages, and whether fees paid on a unit are recoverable. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ESTATE OF KUO-CHANG et al VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant City And County Of San Francisco Motion For Summary Judgment (Continued From Oct-01-2009)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 6, 2009, line 4, Defendant City and County of San Francisco Motion for summary judgment. Motion for summary judgment granted. The City established the elements of design immunity. (Government Code section 830.6). Dusseault declaration and his deposition testimony show that he reviewed the plan and ensured that it complied with both national and city standards regarding the amount of time allocated to an average pedestrian crossing the street. He approved the 2003 signal plan that was in effect at the time of the accident. The Folks declaration underscored the reasonableness of the plan. Substantial evidence of reasonableness demonstrated. Plaintiffs have not established a loss of design immunity by inadmissible evidence of post accident complaints. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: KENDRA TOMCIK DR et al VS. SIDNEY FERNALD DR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 6, 2009, line 8, DEFENDANT SIDNEY FERNALD DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to all causes of action. Plaintiff must allege fraud with particularity, specify which contracts and which terms are at issue for breach of contract cause of action, and how the public is likely to be deceived by alleged unfair business practices. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: REBECCA B. MANCE VS. STATE FARM INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 6, 2009, line 10, DEFENDANT JEFFREY LEVIN MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Motion to strike denied in part and granted in part. Motion denied as to Exhibits A, B, and C, paragraphs 2-6, 11-13, and 56-59. Motion to strike granted as to paragraphs 38-41, 47-48, 50-53, 62-66, 76, and 80 regarding State Farm and settlement negotiations, and page 1, line 14-15, page 32 paragraphs 2 and 3; and page 33 paragraph 11. Plaintiff will amend the complaint within ten days to delete specific amounts for damages, attorney's fees for bringing this tort action, references to State Farm and settlement negotiations, and will name defendant as an individual to the suit. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL A SIMENSTAD VS. INCIDENT REVIEW COMMITTEE OF THE BOARD OF PILOT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Otion For Injunction To Prevent Irreparable Harm From Unlawful Excercise Of Public Statute And Office;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 6, 2009, line 11, PLAINTIFF MICHAEL SIMENSTAD Notice Of Motion And Motion For Injunction To Prevent Irreparable Harm From Unlawful Exercise Of Public Statute And Office. Motion for injunction denied. Defendant did have proper statutory authority to conduct an investigation and make a recommendation to the Board. (Harb. & Nav. section 1180.3) Speeding constitutes misconduct since Harb. & Nav. sec. 1181 is a nonexhaustive list of misconduct for which a license may be suspended. No evidence presented that defendant's investigations were conducted in any unfair or partial manner. Plaintiff cannot show irreparable harm or a probability prevailing on the merits. =(302/CWW)
